<think> I know that the "Luftwaffe" was the air force of Nazi Germany during World War II. A field marshal is a high-ranking military officer. I recall that the most famous German fighter ace of World War I was Manfred von Richthofen, also known as the "Red Baron". However, I'm looking for a distant cousin of a field marshal, not the field marshal himself. I also know that Hermann Göring was a field marshal and a high-ranking officer in the Luftwaffe. I'm not sure if he has any notable relatives who were fighter aces, but I do know that his cousin, Adolf Galland, was a Luftwaffe officer and a fighter ace. However, I'm not sure if he was a distant cousin of a field marshal. I also recall that Werner Mölders was a German fighter ace and a Luftwaffe officer, and he was a distant cousin of Erhard Milch, who was a field marshal. Mölders was credited with 102 victories, but I'm looking for someone with 40 victories. I'm not sure if there's a more direct connection between Mölders and a field marshal, but I do know that he was a prominent figure in the Luftwaffe. After some thought, I'm going to take a guess that Werner Mölders is the distant cousin of a German field marshal I'm looking for. </think>
<answer> Werner Mölders </answer>
